items.front: Delay recording: when does Hold Data Menu or Hold View auto-record?
items.back: When the aircraft is CLEARED OUT OF HOLD. Not when entering holding. Per 13-1-11 NOTE.
Automatically via ERAM Hold Data Menu, Hold View, hold message, OR manually on flight progress strips or facility-approved worksheets. Must follow facility-defined standard. Per 13-1-11(b).

items.front: Overdue aircraft notification: what action is required?
items.back: Take appropriate actions set forth in Chapter 10, Section 3. The notification is based on radar track data; updating the route of flight removes the notification but does NOT replace the required overdue-aircraft response. Per 13-1-12.
Do NOT use GPD for: radar identification, position information, transfer of radar identification, radar separation, correlation, or point outs. Per 13-1-13(d).

items.front: GPD: what is it allowed for?
items.back: ONLY to aid situational awareness and strategic planning. Not a substitute for radar track position, Mode C, or any radar function. Per 13-1-13(a-d).

items.front: Forecast wind data unavailable: what is the rule?
items.back: CONTINUE using conflict probe and trial planning, but with appropriate recognition that alert AND trajectory data may be affected. Per 13-1-14.

items.front: Loss of interfacility connectivity to adjacent ERAM facility: what is the rule?
items.back: CONTINUE use of EDST, but with appropriate recognition that alert data may be affected. Per 13-1-15.
